title: Jennifer Garner posts throwback video of karaoke night at Nantucket restaurant
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Jennifer Garner shared a throwback video of a karaoke night in Nantucket, featuring co-stars from 'The Five-Star Weekend'.
The video shows Garner and co-stars singing 'Dancing Queen' at Gaslight, with local guide Bill Jamieson joining them onstage.
Garner praised Nantucket and highlighted her visits to local attractions during filming for 'The Five-Star Weekend'.

title: Here’s what meteorologists are saying about the yellow skies across the region
author: Jack Pickell
bullets: Wildfire smoke from Canada causes yellow skies in Boston, limiting forecasted daytime heat, National Weather Service reports.
Further southward movement of smoke expected, with potential surface detection and hazy conditions on Wednesday, says NWS Boston.
Meteorologists predict the smoky skies and moderate temperatures may continue through Thursday due to Canadian wildfire smoke.

title: A very special traffic cone arrives in Boston, courtesy of our new friends in Glasgow
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: A special traffic cone from Glasgow, signed by Stuart Patrick, arrived in Boston via a first-class Delta flight.
The cone symbolizes Boston and Glasgow's new partnership, fundraising for mental health resources in both regions.
Massachusetts Gov. Maura Healey and Mayor Michelle Wu will sign the cone during a ceremony at Logan Airport.

title: 3 more MBTA employees charged with overtime fraud, falsifying track inspections
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Three new MBTA employees are charged with overtime fraud and falsifying Red Line track inspection reports at Cabot Yard.
Seven MBTA employees face allegations of working on personal vehicles and false overtime reporting while on duty.
Previously indicted in 2025, the accused include supervisor Brian Pfaffinger and inspectors Ronald Gamble and Magda Trinh.

title: The World Cup was great, but Boston still doesn’t need the Olympics
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: France won the last World Cup quarterfinal held in Foxborough, concluding Boston's World Cup involvement.
Boston's World Cup experience ranked top among U.S. venues, sparking discussions of hosting future major events.
Boston's past Olympic bid included costly projects, leading to skepticism about future hosting due to potential overruns.

title: Bill Belichick’s daughter-in-law wanted for arrest in North Carolina after traffic stop
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: A North Carolina judge issued an arrest warrant for Jen Belichick after she missed a court appearance for speeding.
Jen Belichick was cited for driving 88 mph in a 70 mph zone in Brunswick County, North Carolina.
Bill Belichick, her father-in-law, currently coaches the University of North Carolina football team.

title: Karen Read, Turtleboy look to get witnesses’ defamation suit tossed
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Karen Read and blogger Aidan Kearney seek dismissal of a defamation lawsuit filed by witnesses from Read's murder case.
Witnesses claim Read and Kearney's accusations about their roles in John O'Keefe's death led to harassment and reputational damage.
Read and Kearney argue their actions are protected as free speech under the state's anti-SLAPP law targeting strategic litigation.

title: The invisible force helping fuel this summer’s intense heat, and all our weather
author: David Epstein
bullets: The jet stream, a high-altitude wind current, significantly influences weather patterns, including this summer's intense heat.
The concept of 'quasi-resonant amplification' may partly explain prolonged weather extremes like heat waves, though it's not fully proven.
Current extreme heat advisories stretch from Montana to Maine, linked to the same wavering jet stream pattern.

title: Patriots 2026 position preview: Offensive line edition
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Patriots' offensive line improved in 2025 but remains a major question mark for the 2026 season.
Four of the Patriots' offensive line starters will be 28 or younger, including new center Jared Wilson.
The team allowed 21 sacks in the postseason, facing challenges such as Campbell's pressures in the Super Bowl.

title: New mural brings fresh life to the corner of Doyle’s and the Midway Cafe
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: A new mural in Jamaica Plain commemorates Anthony Bourdain's visit with Barack Obama in Vietnam, reviving the corner by Doyle's.
Watermark Development's Lee Goodman commissioned the mural, featuring Obama and Bourdain, to enliven the area with historical significance.
Artist Lee Beard, known as SOÉMS, created the mural, which coincides with the release of a biopic film about Bourdain.
